title: Erioneuron nealleyi
taxonomy: Erioneuron nealleyi is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Erioneuron, which consists of two species of grasses native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Erioneuron nealleyi is further categorized as a member of the tribe Cynodonteae within the family Poaceae. This classification places it among the various species of grasses characterized by their distinctive growth habits and leaf morphology. Its classification is also supported by molecular studies that have confirmed its phylogenetic relationships within the Poaceae family.
uses: Erioneuron nealleyi, also known as Nealley's grama, is a native grass to North America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its supposed benefits. Additionally, it is believed to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Erioneuron nealleyi for medicinal purposes.

intro: Erioneuron nealleyi, commonly known as Nealey's grama or Nealey's erioneuron, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Erioneuron nealleyi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Erioneuron nealleyi is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds.
